title: Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in R | Stats NZ
content:
## Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in R
16 October 2025, 11:00am
This step-by-step guide shows you how to retrieve data in R using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API.

## Step 1: Get an API key
Sign up to Stats NZ’s API Portal if you haven’t already. In the portal, subscribe to the Aotearoa Data Explorer API and generate a primary key. You’ll need this key in step 4.
API Portal explains how to sign up and create and manage subscription keys.

## Step 2: Determine the URL for the data you want
The data and structural metadata you want needs to be defined in a URL. You’ll need this in step 4.
An easy way to determine a URL is to use the Aotearoa Data Explorer browser to find and customise a table. Then select the Developer API option above the table (2a in the screenshot below) and copy the data-query URL (2b) and structure-query URL (2c).
Aotearoa Data Explorer – API user guide has more detail about URLs.

## Step 3: Install the rsdmx library
Load the rsdmx library if you don’t already have it installed.
The rsdmx quickstart guide has more information about the rsdmx library.

## Step 4: Complete and run the R code
Follow the code snippet below, where [ ] indicates information to be filled in using the details from above.
library(rsdmx) #loads rsdmx library. You need to have installed it first.
library(rsdmx) #loads rsdmx library. You need to have installed it first.
dataurl <- " [url, see (2b) above] "
dataurl <- " [url, see (2b) above] "
metadataUrl <- " [url, see (2c) above] "
metadataUrl <- " [url, see (2c) above] "
key <- " [primary key from (1) above] "
key <- " [primary key from (1) above] "
sdmx_data <- readSDMX(dataurl, verbose = TRUE, headers = c(`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` = key))
sdmx_data <- readSDMX(dataurl, verbose = TRUE, headers = c(`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` = key))
sdmx_metadata <- readSDMX(metadataUrl, verbose = TRUE, headers = c(`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` = key))
sdmx_metadata <- readSDMX(metadataUrl, verbose = TRUE, headers = c(`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` = key))
sdmx_data <- rsdmx:::setDSD(sdmx_data, sdmx_metadata) #align metadata information with the data to generate readable labels using the internal function `setDSD` from the rsdmx library
sdmx_data <- rsdmx:::setDSD(sdmx_data, sdmx_metadata) #align metadata information with the data to generate readable labels using the internal function `setDSD` from the rsdmx library
(data <- as.data.frame(sdmx_data, labels = TRUE)) #using () displays the results in the console.
(data <- as.data.frame(sdmx_data, labels = TRUE)) #using () displays the results in the console.
Now you should be able to work with data and manipulate it in R.
Note: This code does not specify a response format, so the API will return information using the default XML format. If you wish to use another response format such as JSON or CSV, you can specify this as part of the URL in steps 2 and 3. Refer to Specify the response format in the Aotearoa Data Explorer – API user guide. You may need to adjust the code for later steps to work with a different response format.
